sources: ICH Q1A(R2) - Stability Testing of New Drug Substances and Products
ICH Q3A(R2) - Impurities in New Drug Substances
ICH Q3B(R2) - Impurities in New Drug Products
ICH Q5C - Stability Testing of Biotechnological/Biological Products
ICH Q6A - Specifications: Test Procedures and Acceptance Criteria (Chemical)
ICH Q6B - Specifications: Test Procedures and Acceptance Criteria (Biotech)
FDA Guidance - Analytical Procedures and Methods Validation (Jul 2015)
FDA Guidance - Dissolution Testing and Acceptance Criteria (Aug 1997)

sections.S.2: Manufacture (manufacturer, process description, controls, process validation)
sections.S.4: Control of Drug Substance (specifications, analytical procedures, validation, batch analyses, justification)
sections.S.7: Stability (stability summary, post-approval protocol, stability data, commitment)

sections.P.2: Pharmaceutical Development (components, formulation, manufacturing process, container closure, microbiological, compatibility)
sections.P.3: Manufacture (manufacturers, batch formula, process description, controls of critical steps, process validation)
sections.P.4: Control of Excipients (specifications, analytical procedures, validation)
sections.P.5: Control of Drug Product (specifications, analytical procedures, validation, batch analyses, characterisation of impurities, justification)
sections.P.8: Stability (stability summary, post-approval protocol, stability data, commitment)

stability_requirements.source: ICH Q1A(R2), ICH Q5C, FDA Stability Testing NDA/ANDA

intermediate.note: Not required if Option B is long-term condition

frozen.accelerated: No defined condition; test single batch at elevated temperature for excursion assessment

intermediate.note: Not required if Option B is long-term condition

semi_permeable_containers.water_loss_significant_change: 5% loss from initial value after equivalent of 3 months at 40C/NMT 25% RH

biotech_products.drug_substance: Store at proposed conditions (often -20C or below)
biotech_products.drug_product: Store at proposed conditions (typically 2-8C for liquid, ambient for lyophilized)

testing_frequency.long_term: Every 3 months Y1, every 6 months Y2, annually thereafter through proposed shelf life
testing_frequency.intermediate: Minimum 4 time points including initial and final (0, 6, 9, 12 months)
testing_frequency.biotech_shelf_life_lte_1yr: Monthly x3 months, then quarterly through expiry
testing_frequency.biotech_shelf_life_gt_1yr: Quarterly Y1, biannually Y2, annually thereafter

batch_requirements.drug_product_scale: 2 of 3 batches at least pilot scale; third may be smaller if justified
batch_requirements.pilot_scale_definition: 1/10 of production scale OR 100,000 tablets/capsules, whichever is larger (solid oral)
batch_requirements.photostability: At least 1 primary batch per ICH Q1B (>= 1.2 million lux-hours visible + >= 200 W-hr/m2 UV)

significant_change_drug_product: >= 5% change in assay from initial value
Any degradation product exceeding its acceptance criterion
Failure to meet appearance/physical attributes specification
Failure to meet pH acceptance criterion
Failure of 12 dosage units to meet dissolution specification
Failure to meet water content specification

statistical_requirements.confidence_limit: 95% one-sided confidence limit for shelf-life estimation
statistical_requirements.batch_pooling_p_value: > 0.25 for slopes and intercepts to justify combining batch data

shelf_life_extrapolation.maximum_extrapolation: No more than 2x the period covered by long-term data

stability_commitment.when_required: When long-term data on primary batches do not cover proposed shelf life at approval
stability_commitment.drug_product_includes: Long-term studies through shelf life AND accelerated studies for 6 months

drug_substance_organic.scope: New drug substances produced by chemical synthesis

max_daily_dose_lte_2g.identification: 0.10% or 1.0 mg/day intake, whichever is lower
max_daily_dose_lte_2g.qualification: 0.15% or 1.0 mg/day intake, whichever is lower

specification_must_include: Any unspecified impurity with limit <= identification threshold

qualification_studies.genotoxicity: Point mutation AND chromosomal aberration, both in vitro (minimum screen)
qualification_studies.general_toxicity: One or more studies, one species, 14-90 days duration

drug_product_degradation.scope: Degradation products of drug substance or reaction products with excipients/container closure

identification_thresholds.max_daily_dose_lt_1mg: 1.0% or 5 ug TDI, whichever is lower
identification_thresholds.max_daily_dose_1mg_to_10mg: 0.5% or 20 ug TDI, whichever is lower
identification_thresholds.max_daily_dose_gt_10mg_to_2g: 0.2% or 2 mg TDI, whichever is lower

qualification_thresholds.max_daily_dose_lt_10mg: 1.0% or 50 ug TDI, whichever is lower
qualification_thresholds.max_daily_dose_10mg_to_100mg: 0.5% or 200 ug TDI, whichever is lower
qualification_thresholds.max_daily_dose_gt_100mg_to_2g: 0.2% or 3 mg TDI, whichever is lower

batch_documentation_per_batch: Use of batch (clinical studies, stability studies)
Batch number of drug substance used

class_1.description: Known or suspected human carcinogens/toxicants with limited or no use in pharma manufacturing
class_1.action: Must be assessed in all cases; control required if PDE may be exceeded

class_2A.description: Relatively high probability of occurrence in drug product; known toxicity
class_2A.action: Must always be assessed in risk assessment

class_2B.description: Low probability of occurrence unless intentionally added
class_2B.action: Assess if intentionally added or known to be present

class_3.description: Relatively low toxicity by oral route; higher PDEs

elemental_impurities.control_threshold: 30% of PDE -- if estimated level is below 30% of PDE, no additional controls needed

risk_assessment_required_for.class_2B: Only if intentionally added or if known to be present

residual_solvents.class_1: Avoid (known human carcinogens/strongly suspected carcinogens/environmental hazards)

tests.description: Qualitative statement of physical form and color
tests.identification: At least 2 independent methods; one must be specific (IR, HPLC-MS); must discriminate from related compounds
tests.assay: Mass balance or specific stability-indicating procedure; typically 98.0-102.0%
tests.impurities_inorganic: Pharmacopeial or validated methods; per Q3D

tests.identification: At least 2 methods (one specific) for single-active; two specific for multi-active
tests.assay: Specific, stability-indicating; typically 90.0-110.0% label claim (oral solids)
tests.uniformity_of_dosage_units: USP <905>; L1 limit AV <= 15.0, L2 limit AV <= 25.0
tests.water_content: Karl Fischer or LOD where appropriate

drug_substance_specification.identity: At least 2 orthogonal methods (peptide map + one other)
drug_substance_specification.purity: Orthogonal methods (SEC + IEX or CE-SDS + SEC)
drug_substance_specification.impurities_product_related: Aggregates (SEC-HPLC), fragments (reduced CE-SDS), charge variants (IEX or icIEF)

impurities_process_related.residual_DNA: qPCR or Threshold, typically <= 10 ng/dose per WHO

drug_substance_specification.potency: Quantitative biological activity assay with dose-response

drug_product_additional.sterility: Membrane filtration or direct inoculation per pharmacopeia
drug_product_additional.endotoxin: LAL or recombinant Factor C; <= 5 EU/kg/hr

decision_trees.DT1: Impurity acceptance criteria for specified impurities
decision_trees.DT2: Particle size: required if bioavailability affected, or inhalation/suspension product
decision_trees.DT3: Polymorphism: control if different polymorphs affect solubility and biopharmaceutics
decision_trees.DT4: Chirality: control if enantiomer has different safety/activity profile
decision_trees.DT5: Microbial quality: TAMC/TYMC limits for non-sterile products
decision_trees.DT6: Water content: specify if hygroscopic or if water affects stability/performance
decision_trees.DT7: Dissolution: rapidly dissolving (>80% in 15 min) and highly soluble (dose/250 mL) may justify disintegration only
decision_trees.DT7.1: Extended-release dissolution: at least 3 time points, variability +/- 10% of mean
decision_trees.DT8: Parametric release: may replace sterility test if validated sterilization with Fmin >= 8

dissolution_and_bioequivalence.source: FDA Dissolution Testing, FDA Bioequivalence Studies

class_1.criteria: Highest dose dissolves in <= 250 mL, pH 1.0-8.0, 37C; >= 90% absorbed

dissolution_definitions.rapid: >= 85% dissolved in <= 30 minutes (USP App I at 100 rpm or App II at 50 rpm, <= 900 mL, in 0.1N HCl, pH 4.5, pH 6.8)

rules: Only use time points where both profiles have mean dissolution < 85%
Only one time point after 85% dissolution should be used

requirements: Both test and reference demonstrate rapid dissolution (>= 85% in 30 min) in all 3 media
NOT a narrow therapeutic index drug

specification_setting.bcs_class_1: Q = 80% (NLT 85% dissolved); time point <= 60 min (preferably 30 min)

analytical_validation.source: FDA Analytical Procedures Validation, ICH Q2(R1)

specificity.requirement: Measure analyte unequivocally in presence of degradation products, excipients, impurities
specificity.acceptance: Peak purity confirmed; no interference; forced degradation demonstrating mass balance

linearity.requirement: Direct proportional relationship between response and concentration
linearity.acceptance: r >= 0.999; y-intercept within +/- 2% of 100% response; minimum 5 concentration levels

precision_repeatability.requirement: Same analyst, same day, same instrument

activities: Establishment of proven acceptable ranges or design space

part_a_facility.IQ: Verify equipment installed per design specifications
part_a_facility.OQ: Verify equipment operates across anticipated operating ranges
part_a_facility.PQ: Demonstrate equipment performs reproducibly under production conditions
part_a_facility.utilities: HVAC, water systems (WFI, PW), compressed gases, clean steam

protocol_must_specify: Manufacturing conditions including CPPs and ranges
Data to be collected and timing
part_b_PPQ.minimum_batches: Typically 3 consecutive successful batches; more for complex processes
part_b_PPQ.sampling: Enhanced; more intensive than routine production

activities: Trend analysis (X-bar, R charts, CUSUM, EWMA)

required_testing: Complete extractables profile (multiple solvents: aqueous, acidic, basic, organic)
Leachables study using actual drug product
Toxicological evaluation of all identified extractables
USP Biological Reactivity In Vivo (Class VI) and In Vitro
USP Chemical Resistance (Glass Type I, II, or III)

glass_classification.type_I_borosilicate: Required for most injectables; highest chemical resistance

required_content.P.2.1: Components of Drug Product: compatibility studies, excipient justification, functional roles, overages
required_content.P.2.2: Drug Product: formulation development rationale, clinical vs. commercial formulation differences
required_content.P.2.3: Manufacturing Process Development: selection/optimization rationale, CPPs, scale-up, in-process controls
required_content.P.2.4: Container Closure System: suitability, extraction/interaction, protection, compatibility, functionality
required_content.P.2.5: Microbiological Attributes: preservative effectiveness, water activity

qbd_elements.QTPP: Prospective summary of quality characteristics (dosage form, route, strength, container closure, release criteria, stability)
qbd_elements.CQAs: Physical, chemical, biological, or microbiological property that must be within limits
qbd_elements.design_space: Multidimensional combination of input variables demonstrated to provide quality assurance; working within = not a change

control_strategy_tiers.tier_2: In-process testing at critical steps + reduced end-product testing

comparability_protocols.purpose: Prospectively defined plan for post-approval CMC changes with pre-agreed acceptance criteria
requirements: Specific, bounded description of proposed changes (not open-ended)
Scientific rationale for change and expected comparability
Risk assessment of impact on identity, strength, quality, purity, potency
Complete tests and studies plan with prospective acceptance criteria
Contingency plan if criteria not met

filing_category_benefits.PAS_to_CBE30: Well-constructed CP may reduce Prior Approval Supplement to CBE-30

acceptance_criteria_principles: Must be prospectively defined before change
Scientifically justified from batch history (15-20 commercial batches preferred)
Narrower than or equal to approved specifications
Dissolution: f2 >= 50 across all comparison media
Assay: within +/- 2-3% of batch history mean
Stability: accelerated + long-term on post-change product

stability_to_impurities.Q1A_to_Q3A: Stress testing identifies potential degradation impurities
stability_to_impurities.Q1A_to_Q3B: Degradation products observed on stability must be reported per Q3B thresholds

stability_to_specifications.Q1A_to_Q6A: Stability data informs shelf-life specifications and release vs. shelf-life acceptance criteria
stability_to_specifications.Q5C_to_Q6B: Stability-indicating profile uses Q6B specification tests

development_to_specifications.Q8_to_Q6B: CQA risk assessment for biotech product specifications

impurities_to_specifications.Q3A_to_Q6A_DT1: Decision Tree #1 for establishing acceptance criteria for specified impurities
impurities_to_specifications.Q3B_to_Q6A_DT2: Decision Tree #2 for establishing acceptance criteria for specified degradation products

elemental_to_specifications.Q3D_to_Q6A: Elemental impurity limits feed into drug substance and drug product specifications

process_validation_to_development.FDA_PV_to_Q8: Design space, CQAs, knowledge-based approach inform process validation strategy

analytical_to_all.Q2_to_Q3A_Q3B: Validated methods required for impurity detection at specified thresholds
analytical_to_all.Q2_to_Q6A_Q6B: All specification tests require validated analytical procedures

key_regulatory_citations.FDC_Act_501a2B: Adulteration - failure to conform to CGMP
